Limitation of Liability.standardDescription: Liability is capped at the fees paid or payable in the 12 months preceding the claim. Excludes indirect, consequential, or punitive damages.

Termination.standardDescription: Either party can terminate for convenience with 30 days written notice. Immediate termination for material breach if uncured within 30 days.

Confidentiality.standardDescription: Mutual obligations of confidentiality applying equally to both parties, with standard exceptions (public domain, prior knowledge, legal compulsion).

Intellectual Property.standardDescription: Each party retains ownership of its background intellectual property. Developed IP/deliverables assigned to customer upon full payment.

Force Majeure.standardDescription: Excuses performance for standard events beyond reasonable control (natural disasters, acts of God, war). Requires immediate notice and mitigation.

Data Protection.standardDescription: Commitment to comply with applicable data protection laws (GDPR, CCPA), including security measures, data breach notification within 72 hours, and data return/destruction.

Indemnity.standardDescription: Provider indemnifies customer for third-party intellectual property claims. Customer indemnifies provider for claims arising from unauthorized use.

Governing Law.standardDescription: Governing law of a major neutral jurisdiction (e.g., Delaware, New York, or the state where the defendant is located) without regard to conflicts of law.

Payment Terms.standardDescription: Payment due within 30 days of invoice receipt. Disputed amounts may be withheld in good faith, and interest on late payments capped at a reasonable rate (e.g., 1-1.5% per month).

Dispute Resolution.standardDescription: Escalation process starting with executive negotiations, followed by mediation, and final binding arbitration under AAA or JAMS rules.

Non-Compete.standardDescription: Non-compete obligations limited in scope, geography, and duration (maximum of 12 months post-termination) to protect legitimate business interests.

Assignment.standardDescription: Neither party may assign the contract without the other party's written consent, except in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all assets.

Warranty.standardDescription: Services performed in a professional, workmanlike manner conforming to industry standards and specifications. Reperformance as sole remedy within 90 days.

Audit Rights.standardDescription: Audit permitted once per year during business hours with at least 15 business days' notice. Auditor must sign a confidentiality agreement, and cost is borne by auditor unless a material discrepancy is found.

Insurance.standardDescription: Maintain standard insurance policies: Commercial General Liability ($1M/$2M), Professional Liability/Errors & Omissions ($1M-$2M), and Worker's Compensation.
